{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19255","id":2719526847,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6iGK-_","number":19255,"title":"[Help wanted","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-05T06:59:17Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T06:59:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Galaxy Australia is considering adding S3 storage for some user data. The purpose would be for the storage of data that is not newly created, maybe 3+ months since last access. We have been testing several options (object store, MinIO and an AWS bucket). We are running tests of how Galaxy handles access to files that are not in the cache. We are testing running jobs on datasets that are not in the cache, and downloading datasets that are not in the cache.

Results from running jobs are excellent on the local cluster and on pulsar - the jobs end up in ‘ok’ state.

Results from downloading files are concerning. I have been testing this with file sizes of 2GB, 4GB, 6GB, 8GB and 10GB. Two of the backends we are testing (ids `minio_test` and `aws_bucket_test` in object store conf) have type boto3 and only the downloads of 2GB and 4GB files are successfully downloaded. For anything larger, attempting to download leads to timeout (504). Dataset peek is similarly affected.

For the backend connected to openstack object storage (id `pawsey_s3_test` in our object store conf) we have used backend type `generic_s3`. The downloads appear to succeed, but for sizes 4GB and above, the downloaded files are truncated to about 3GB each. It would be preferable if it failed with a timeout as it does in the other cases.

At this point our test candidates seem unusable, especially the one in which downloaded files are truncated.

I’m keen to talk to others who are using or testing S3 in the Galaxy object store.

This is a link to our [object store conf](https://github.com/usegalaxy-au/infrastructure/blob/master/templates/galaxy/config/galaxy_object_store_conf.yml.j2#L174C1-L259C47) and I've pasted the relevant entries below.

```
- id: minio_test
  weight: 0
  type: boto3
  store_by: uuid
  auth:
    access_key: {{ vault_galaxy_minio_access_key }}
    secret_key: {{ vault_galaxy_minio_secret_key }}
  bucket:
    name: galaxytest
  connection:
    endpoint_url: https://platforms-s3-poc.aarnet.edu.au
  cache:
    path: /mnt/user-data-volD/minio_test_cache
    size: 50
    cache_updated_data: true
  # transfer:   # see transfer options for boto3 above in AWS configuration.
- id: aws_bucket_test
  type: boto3
  weight: 0
  store_by: uuid
  auth:
    access_key: {{ vault_aws_galaxytest_access_key }}
    secret_key: {{ vault_aws_galaxytest_secret_key }}
  bucket:
    name: ga-s3-test-230823
  connection:  # not strictly needed but more of the API works with this.
    region: ap-southeast-2
  cache:
    path: /mnt/user-data-volD/aws_bucket_test_cache
    size: 50
    cache_updated_data: true
- id: pawsey_s3_test
  type: generic_s3
  weight: 0
  store_by: uuid
  auth:
    access_key: {{ vault_pawsey_s3_test_access_key }}
    secret_key: {{ vault_pawsey_s3_test_secret_key }}
  bucket:
    name: galaxy-test
    use_reduced_redundancy: false
    max_chunk_size: 250
  connection:
    host: "projects.pawsey.org.au"
    port: 443
    is_secure: true
    conn_path: "/"
  cache:
    path: /mnt/user-data-volD/pawsey_s3_test_cache
    size: 50
    cache_updated_data: true
```

**Expected behavior**
A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more restricted query for fastqc is now scalable cores and memory based off input_size - probably I should fix this for compressed data a la [my bwa-mem2 rule](https://github.com/galaxyproject/usegalaxy-playbook/blob/6341fbc7d5d0649d00c66dcc10b45d7f16f113a7/env/common/templates/galaxy/config/tpv/tools.yaml.j2#L1116) but perhaps TPV should have an `estimated_input_size`?

Data I used to make adjustments can be found [in this gist](https://gist.github.com/natefoo/18df3574537082ec2f5f85698acbf551). There is also a discussion about how to do a better job of this long term in #75.

Any tool I didn't have data for that has `cores` but no `mem` I left a comment on. fastp I don't know what to do with, since its usage is way outside of what I allocate for it, so I might try a more am not sure what is going on here but:

- [AU allocates](https://github.com/usegalaxy-au/infrastructure/blob/549c324bdbae7502f630cdc147dd4147918bbcf2/files/galaxy/dynamic_job_rules/production/total_perspective_vortex/tools.yml#L537) from 11.5 to 30.7 GB and multiple cores, although I don't believe the wrapper supports multiple cores?
- In the shared DB (and on .org) we allocate 34 GB.

In practice on .org the histogram looks like:

```console
ndc@galaxy-db% gxadmin tsvquery tool-metrics 'toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/devteam/clustalw/clustalw/2.1+galaxy1' memory.peak --ok | awk '{print $1 / 1024 / 1024 / 1024}' | gxadmin filter histogram

Maybe old versions were much more inefficient? But it does not seem like we need to allocate anything special for the current version. I would be interested to see what memory usage looks like at EU and AU.

Also with larger inputs the tool runs forever, AU rejects anything >= 40 MB, I will update if I can determine what the rough size at which it runs forever for us is, and maybe it is worth including that in the shared DB?

raxml wants 16 cores but because of the memory factor that most people use in their default tool (see #73) is probably going to also request ~64 GB of memory despite only using ~2 GB:

```console
ndc@galaxy-db% gxadmin tsvquery tool-metrics 'toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/raxml/raxml/8.2%' memory.peak --ok --like | awk '{print $1 / 1024 / 1024 / 1024}' | gxadmin filter histogram

am not sure what is going on here but:

- [AU allocates](https://github.com/usegalaxy-au/infrastructure/blob/549c324bdbae7502f630cdc147dd4147918bbcf2/files/galaxy/dynamic_job_rules/production/total_perspective_vortex/tools.yml#L537) from 11.5 to 30.7 GB and multiple cores, although I don't believe the wrapper supports multiple cores?
- In the shared DB (and on .org) we allocate 34 GB.

Maybe old versions were much more inefficient? But it does not seem like we need to allocate anything special for the current version. I would be interested to see what memory usage looks like at EU and AU.

Also with larger inputs the tool runs forever, AU rejects anything >= 40 MB, I will update if I can determine what the rough size at which it runs forever for us is, and maybe it is worth including that in the shared DB?

raxml wants 16 cores but because of the memory factor that most people use in their default tool (see #73) is probably going to also request ~64 GB of memory despite only using ~2 GB:

our previous entries have gone in the `gtdbtk_database` loc file.

This is the content of the `gtdbtk_database` data table:

```
value | name | path
-- | -- | --
release_207 | gtdbtk database release 207 | /mnt/custom-indices/gtdbtk_database/gtdb_tk_data_20220408
release202 | gtdbtk database release 202 | /mnt/custom-indices/gtdbtk_database/release202
release_207_v2 | gtdbtk database release 207_v2 | /mnt/custom-indices/gtdbtk_database/release_207_v2
```

The tool help text says `This version of GTDB-Tk requires GTDB version 207 or 214`

What I'm wondering is, can I make an entry in the `gtdbtk_database_versioned` that points to one of our 207 databases and if so, which one should I choose: 207 or 207_v2?

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18636","id":2443824662,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6Rqc4W","number":18636,"title":"Editing a group through admin UI can lead to duplication of user_group_association entries","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-02T02:01:37Z","updated_at":"2024-08-02T02:01:37Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
The number of user_group_assocation entries for Galaxy Australia’s most populous user group increased from 3,000 to 19,000 in the space of 10 minutes. In the nginx logs there were 6 requests submitted within 3 minutes of `PUT /admin/manage_users_and_roles_for_group?id=2b32d97164233881` (the same group ID) and all of these requests timed out.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Australia, release_24.1
`3be367228b531c346c10700f07d57ae44394be36`

**To Reproduce**
As admin
1) Add 1000 members to a user group. (Admin -> Groups -> <Group Name> -> Edit Permissions) and save group.
2) Add another user
3) Click Save a few times - the button is not disabled
4) Check db for multiple user_group_association entries `select user_id, group_id, count(id) from user_group_association where group_id = <group_id> group by user_id, group_id having count(id) > 1;`

**Expected behavior**
If a user_group_association exists for user X, group Y another one will not be created.

Steps to reproduce:
(1) Upload a dataset
(2) Look at file size on the dataset info page
(3) Delete the dataset permanently
(4) Refresh the dataset info page. The dataset size is not '-'

Steps to reproduce:
(1) Upload a dataset
(2) Look at file size on the dataset info page
(3) Delete the dataset permanently
(4) Refresh the dataset info page. The dataset size is now '-'

I think this must be new because I look at past job data frequently for the purpose of updating TPV memory allocations. I only recently started seeing jobs with 0 bytes of input.

Not sure if this is a bug.

Dataset List 'Copy to Current History' action on a collection element results in galaxy making a copy of a hda with the same ID as the hdca selected. For admin users, this means a copy of an old hda belonging to another user turns up in current history. If the user is not admin, the action fails with the error "Error: HistoryDatasetAssociation is not accessible by user"

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Australia and Galaxy Europe, release_24.1

**To Reproduce**
Data -> Datasets, choose one that has a blank extension column
From menu on LHS of item name, select 'Copy to current history'
There will either be an error or a hda will appear in your current history (this might be a deleted hda).

**Expected behavior**
A copy of the selected dataset collection will appear in current history

the bug**
The number of user_group_assocation entries for Galaxy Australia’s most populous user group increased from 3,000 to 19,000 in the space of 10 minutes. In the nginx logs there were 6 requests submitted within 3 minutes of `PUT /admin/manage_users_and_roles_for_group?id=2b32d97164233881` (the same group ID) and all of these requests timed out.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Australia, release_24.1
`3be367228b531c346c10700f07d57ae44394be36`

**To Reproduce**
As admin
1) Add 1000 members to a user group. (Admin -> Groups -> *Group Name*-> Edit Permissions) and save group.
2) Add another user
3) Click Save a few times - the button is not disabled
4) Check db for multiple user_group_association entries `select user_id, group_id, count(id) from user_group_association where group_id = <group_id> group by user_id, group_id having count(id) > 1;`

**Expected behavior**
If a user_group_association exists for user X, group Y another one will not be created.

+##############
+Inner workings
+##############
+
+Types of objects
+================
+
+Conceptually, TPV consists of the following types of objects.
+
+1. **Entities** - An entity is anything that will be considered for scheduling
+by TPV. Entities include Tools, Users, Groups, Rules and Destinations.
+All entities have some common properties (id, cores, mem, env, params,
+and scheduling tags).
+
+2. **Scheduling Tags** - Entities can have scheduling tags defined on them that determine which
+entities match up, and which destination they can schedule on. Tags fall into one of four categories,

DATA_FETCH jobs run out of memory. The jobs that use excessive memory are autodectecting the data types, particularly from local files with .qza extensions. We have raised the amount of memory allocated to 12GB per job and this is still not enough for some jobs.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Australia, release_23.1
Commit: a3c14ea

the bug**
If *no* requirements for a tool are resolved through conda, that is considered a resolution miss and the resolution process continues with the next resolver. However, if *any* requirement is found via conda for a tool with multiple requirements and there is not a mulled environment present, Galaxy will attempt to create a per-job environment for that tool, regardless of whether `auto_install` is `false`. As far as I can tell, this cannot be disabled.

In the past I'd make sure I had mulled envs in CVMFS for every tool, but now that we've got all new tool versions using Singularity, we're running in to cases where a tool has a matching biocontainers image, but some of its requirements (but not the correct mulled env) might also be installed individually through conda. On the Galaxy side this is ok as long as you explicitly send those tools to destinations where normal dependency resolution is disabled, but it breaks on the Pulsar side unless you set `dependency_resolution` on that destination (in the Galaxy job conf) to `local`.

So there are some workarounds if you're aware what's going on, but... I am not a big fan of per-job environments and would be happy to see support for them removed. I don't know if people have had success with them, but basically my issues with them are:

1. Incredibly slow env creation on a per-job basis, there is essentially no caching or reuse.
2. You are exporting the list of packages and specific versions in each of the deps that *are* installed to install them in the per-job environment, but those deps at those versions may no longer be installable.
3. If you're lucky, things "appear to work" to the user/admin until one day the requirements can no longer be solved or downloaded, at which point the tool is broken and very difficult to fix.

IMO we are much better off just dropping them entirely. If you have `auto_install` true, it should attempt to install a mulled env. If not, it should fail resolution and (potentially) fail the entire tool.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
all

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install a tool with multiple requirements with a conda resolver enabled, but do not install the requirements.
5. Run the tool, notice that nothing resolves but the tool executes (and fails if things needed aren't on `$PATH`).
6. Install *one*, but not all (mulled) of the dependencies.
7. Run the tool, notice that a per-job environment is created.

**Expected behavior**
If nothing else, `auto_install` is respected or a new option can be added to control this behavior.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13711/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13711/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/comments/1928553863","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13711#issuecomment-1928553863","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/13711","id":1928553863,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAdw-Nc5y82WH","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-06T00:22:43Z","updated_at":"2024-02-06T00:22:43Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"This has showed up on Galaxy Australia a few times, most recently with dbbuilder https://github.com/galaxyproteomics/tools-galaxyp/issues/739. the bug**
Upon a fresh installation of the galaxy virtual environment, galaxy is broken due to this error:
`Exception: FilesSource plugin is missing required Python PyFilesystem2 plugin package [fs.dropboxfs]`

This isn't pinned and the latest version was installed (1.0.1) in which the way DropboxFS is imported has changed. We fixed it by pip installing an older version, 0.2.2.post2.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: release_22.05
Commit: (run `git rev-parse HEAD` if you run this Galaxy server)

updating this role to version 0.10.19 our themes have disappeared, along with the option to select a theme in user preferences.

In our galaxy.yml file we have

```
    themes_config_file: /mnt/galaxy/config/themes_conf.yml
    themes_config_file_by_host: {}
```

changes to job-destinations and ansible-galaxy admin tutorials to prevent ansible-playbook errors.","user":{"login":"Edmontosaurus","id":140177792,"node_id":"U_kgDOCFrxgA","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/140177792?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus","html_url":"https://github.com/Edmontosaurus","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/Edmontosaurus/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Following the job-destinations and ansible-galaxy instructions as they currently are, will both result in failed ansible playbook runs. See the details about the problems and my proposed fixed below. If my proposed fixes are not ideal, then feel free to reject them and just use this pull-request as informational messages about current problems in these tutorials:

* ansible-galaxy tutorial: Following this tutorial will result in ansible playbook errors at line 926 where the variable "miniconda_version: 4.12.0" is defined. This tutorial used to work without errors but recently the miniconda role fails when miniconda_version 4.12 is defined. The cause of this is because the miniconda role by default attempts to install the latest conda version and then downgrades to the version defined in "miniconda_version". The latest miniconda versions can't be downgraded to 4.12 because a 'UnsatisfiableError' error will occur because of python version constraints. (the latest conda version being installed by the role uses python 3.11 which causes the version constraint error.) One workaround for this problem is to define extra variables, as can be seen in the diff. Two other possible solutions are to have the playbook install the latest conda version or to modify the code of the miniconda_role.

* job-destinations tutorial: In this tutorial, the student is instructed to run the ansible playbook after having added the usegalaxy_eu.tpv_auto_lint role. However running this playbook at this point will also result in ansible errors. This happens because TPV itself has not yet been installed. Skipping the playbook run at this point and only running it after the "Configuring TPV" section proved to be a workaround for me. As mentioned in the tutorial, adding TPV as a runner will cause Galaxy to automatically install TPV. The tpv_auto_lint role will only work at this point because it requires TPV to be installed. My proposed fix would be to remove the instruction to run the ansible-playbook at this point and run it after the "Configuring TPV" section.

of deprecated stuff we are currently using:

- [ ] [PEP 594](https://peps.python.org/pep-0594/) led to the deprecations of the following modules slated for removal in Python 3.13: [imghdr](https://docs.python.org/3/library/imghdr.html#module-imghdr)
- [ ] [datetime.datetime](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime)’s [utcnow()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.utcnow) and [utcfromtimestamp()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp) are deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Instead, use timezone-aware objects to represent datetimes in UTC: respectively, call [now()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.now) and [fromtimestamp()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp) with the tz parameter set to [datetime.UTC](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.UTC).
- [ ] Deprecated the `importlib.abc.Traversable` class, scheduled for removal in Python 3.14. Use [importlib.resources.abc.Traversable](https://docs.python.org/3/library/importlib.resources.abc.html#importlib.resources.abc.Traversable) class instead.
- [ ] The [lib2to3](https://docs.python.org/3/library/2to3.html#module-lib2to3) package and [2to3](https://docs.python.org/3/library/2to3.html#to3-reference) tool are now deprecated and may not be able to parse Python 3.10 or newer. See [PEP 617](https://peps.python.org/pep-0617/), introducing the new PEG parser, for details.
- [ ] [tarfile](https://docs.python.org/3/library/tarfile.html#module-tarfile): Extracting tar archives without specifying filter is deprecated until Python 3.14, when 'data' filter will become the default. See [Extraction filters](https://docs.python.org/3/library/tarfile.html#tarfile-extraction-filter) for details.

Others we may be using:
- [ ] Remove the `filename` attribute of [gzip](https://docs.python.org/3/library/gzip.html#module-gzip)’s [gzip.GzipFile](https://docs.python.org/3/library/gzip.html#gzip.GzipFile), deprecated since Python 2.6, use the [name](https://docs.python.org/3/library/gzip.html#gzip.GzipFile.name) attribute instead. In write mode, the `filename` attribute added '.gz' file extension if it was not present.

More are listed at https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.11.html#deprecated and {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16987","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16987/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16987/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16987/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16987","id":1982830666,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc52L5hK","number":16987,"title":"Startup messages at ERROR level when duplicate loc file entries are found","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-11-08T06:20:09Z","updated_at":"2023-11-08T06:20:09Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**
There are a bunch of errors when galaxy starts up and a lot of them have to do with multiple versions of a tool (i.e. featurecounts) having provided the same .loc file, whose entries are read to populate the data tables, but galaxy logs an ERROR if it encounters the same entry twice. For example:

```
Nov 08 05:11:14 galaxy-handlers galaxyctl[1262706]: galaxy.tool_util.data ERROR 2023-11-08 05:11:14,513 [pN:handler_0,p:1262706,tN:MainThread] Attempted to add fields (['hg38', 'hg38']) to data table 'featurecounts_anno', but this entry already exists and allow_duplicates is False
```

I can comment out a segment of the shed_tool_data_table_conf.xml so that there are no duplicates, but as soon as another version of featurecounts is installed there would be another set of errors about duplicate entries.

`ERROR` level seems like overkill for this kind of issue, and perhaps `WARNING` would be more appropriate. The reason that such a change would help us is that we have been starting to use sentry, and too many errors in startup logs leads to a really slow startup.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Australia, release 23.1

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16840","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16840/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16840/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16840/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/16840","id":1940061431,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc5zovz3","number":16840,"title":"`watch_job_rules: true` is causing huge memory consumption","user":{"login":"bgruening","id":469983,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2OTk4Mw==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/469983?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening","html_url":"https://github.com/bgruening","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/bgruening/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2023-10-12T13:55:59Z","updated_at":"2023-10-12T14:27:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"**Describe the bug**

If we run with `watch_job_rules: true` we see a memory usage of up to 30GB per handler.

More information including a py-syp trace is here: https://gist.github.com/bgruening/7be19830e254b4bca99a3ae2e3844041

Disabling the watchdog, as done here, https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/infrastructure-playbook/pull/937 is solving the problem for us.

![grafik](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/469983/4f36cb1f-e719-452d-8566-798ae88c4cd7)


Feel free to close this issue, if this seems to be a EU-specific thing. We are also using TPV, so ping also @nuwang and @cat-bro.

have talked about injecting the values of 'cores', 'mem' and 'gpus' into any job processed by TPV so that it can be queried later, probably into the destination_params field.

It's not possible at the moment to write an SQL query that will extract this info from both Galaxy Australia and Galaxy Europe since these values are stored in different places. For example, Galaxy Australia has
```
{"nativeSpecification": "--nodes=1 --ntasks=4 --ntasks-per-node=4 --mem=16384 --partition=main"}
```
within the encoded destination_params field, and we can extract these values in a query with regex, but the same regex would not work for another Galaxy instance that was either not using slurm or using it differently.

Having values within destination_params (`tpv_cores`, `tpv_mem`, `tpv_gpus`) in addition to our various DRM specifications would be helpful from an admin perspective, and these could also be used in custom ranking functions.

```
May 23 21:35:51
May 27 07:04:13

Jun 06 13:32:08

Jun 21 17:38:30

The time 07:04 is most critical but there's nothing unusual in the logs. The pulsar service is restarted by cron daily at 6:11. @mira-miracoli didn't find anything relevant in the mq.galaxyproject.eu logs.

to @cat-bro as it was noticed on UseGalaxy.org.au, but I think it's a more general issue. One user's username includes a space. It's blocking access to their histories.

- https://usegalaxy.org.au/u/Wossen Mengesha/h/metabarcodingedna-through-obitools

- https://usegalaxy.org.au/u/Wossen%20Mengesha/h/metabarcodingedna-through-obitools

all 404.

**Diagnosis**

You cannot manually enter a space, it's coming from their external authentication system most likely. 

**Remediation**

We should fix it on the external auth side. -## Running with more resources
-
-We want our tool to run with more than one core. To do this, we need to instruct Slurm to allocate more cores for this job. This is done in the job configuration file.
+## Configuring TPV
 
+We want our tool to run with more than one core. To do this, we need to instruct Slurm to allocate more cores for this job. First however, we need to configure Galaxy to use TPV.
 
-> <hands-on-title>Allocating more resources</hands-on-title>
+> <hands-on-title>Adding TPV to your job configuration</hands-on-title>
 >
-> 1. Edit your `templates/galaxy/config/job_conf.yml.j2` and add the following destination. Then, map the new tool to the new destination using the tool ID (`<tool id="testing">`) and destination id (`<destination id="slurm-2c">`) by adding a new section to the job config, `<tools>`, below the destinations:
+> 1. Edit `group_vars/galaxyservers.yml` and add the following destination under your `job_config` section to route all jobs to TPV.

-## Testing the DTD
+# Basic access controls
 
-Our rule specified that any invocation of the `testing` tool with an input dataset with size <16 bytes would run on the 1 core destination, whereas any with >= 16 bytes would run on the 2 core destination.
+You may wish to apply some basic restrictions on which users are allowed to run specific tools. TPV accomodates user and role specific rules. In addition, TPV supports tagging of tools, users, roles and destinations. These tags
+can be matched up so that only desired combinations are compatible with each other. While these mechanisms are detailed in the TPV documentation, we will choose a different problem that highlights some other capabilities
+- restricting a tool so that only an admin can execute that tool.
 
-> <hands-on-title>Testing the DTD</hands-on-title>
->
-> 1. Create a dataset using the upload paste tool with a few (<16) characters
+> <hands-on-title>Using conditionals to restrict a tool to admins only</hands-on-title>
 >

+> 2. Edit your `files/galaxy/config/tpv_rules_local.yml` and add the following rule.

-## Testing the DTD
+# Basic access controls
 
-Our rule specified that any invocation of the `testing` tool with an input dataset with size <16 bytes would run on the 1 core destination, whereas any with >= 16 bytes would run on the 2 core destination.
+You may wish to apply some basic restrictions on which users are allowed to run specific tools. TPV accomodates user and role specific rules. In addition, TPV supports tagging of tools, users, roles and destinations. These tags
+can be matched up so that only desired combinations are compatible with each other. While these mechanisms are detailed in the TPV documentation, we will choose a different problem that highlights some other capabilities
+- restricting a tool so that only an admin can execute that tool.
 
-> <hands-on-title>Testing the DTD</hands-on-title>
->
-> 1. Create a dataset using the upload paste tool with a few (<16) characters
+> <hands-on-title>Using conditionals to restrict a tool to admins only</hands-on-title>
 >
-> 2. Create a dataset using the upload paste tool with >16 characters
+> 2. Edit your `files/galaxy/config/tpv_rules_local.yml` and add the following rule.
 >
-> 3. Run the `Testing Tool` on both datasets.
+>    {% raw %}
+>    ```diff
+>    --- a/files/galaxy/config/tpv_rules_local.yml
+>    +++ b/files/galaxy/config/tpv_rules_local.yml
+>    @@ -8,6 +8,15 @@ tools:
+>         mem: cores * 4
+>       testing:
+>         cores: 2
+>    +    rules:
+>    +      - id: admin_only_testing_tool
+>    +        if: |
+>    +          import os
+>    +          # Only allow the tool to be executed if the user is an admin
+>    +          admin_users = app.config.get( "admin_users", "" ).split( "," )
+>    +          # last line in block must evaluate to a value - which determines whether the TPV if conditional matches or not
+>    +          user_email not in admin_users","path":"topics/admin/tutorials/job-destinations/tutorial.md","commit_id":"52aca673b8d366df765094ca6623a5d8232f0390","original_commit_id":"52aca673b8d366df765094ca6623a5d8232f0390","user":{"login":"cat-bro","id":22670687,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyNjcwNjg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22670687?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro","html_url":"https://github.com/cat-bro","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/cat-bro/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/bioblend/pulls/comments/1168240654","pull_request_review_id":1387358976,"id":1168240654,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAEWDfM5FofAO","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,195 @@
+"""
+Contains interactions dealing with Galaxy container resolvers.
+"""
+from typing import (
+    List,
+    Optional,
+)
+
+from bioblend.galaxy.client import Client
+
+
+class ContainerResolutionClient(Client):
+    module = "container_resolvers"
+
+    def get_container_resolvers(self) -> list:
+        """GET /api/container_resolvers"""
+        url = self._make_url()
+        return self._get(url=url)
+
+    def show_container_resolver(self, index: int) -> dict:
+        """GET /api/container_resolvers/<index>"""
+        url = f"{self._make_url()}/{index}"
+        return self._get(url=url)
+
+    def resolve(
+        self,
+        index: Optional[int] = None,
+        tool_id: Optional[str] = None,
+        resolver_type: Optional[str] = None,
+        container_type: Optional[str] = None,
+        requirements_only: bool = False,
+        install: bool = False,
+    ) -> dict:
+        """
+        Resolve described requirement against specified container resolvers.
+
+        :type index: int
+        :param index: index of the dependency resolver with respect to
+            the dependency resolvers config file
+
+        :type tool_id: str
+        :param tool_id: tool id to resolve against containers
+
+        :type resolver_type: str
+        :param resolver_type: restrict to specified resolver type
+
+        :type container_type: str
+        :param container_type: restrict to specified container type
+
+        :type requirements_only: bool
+        :param requirements_only: ignore tool containers, properties - just search based on tool requirements set to True to mimic default behavior of tool dependency API.
+
+        :type install: bool
+        :param install: allow installation of new containers (for build_mulled containers) the way job resolution will operate, defaults to False
+
+        :rtype: dict
+        TODO: example
+        """
+        params = {}
+        if tool_id:
+            params["tool_id"] = tool_id
+        if resolver_type:
+            params["resolver_type"] = resolver_type
+        if container_type:
+            params["container_type"] = container_type
+        params["requirements_only"] = requirements_only
+        params["install"] = install
+        if index is not None:
+            url = "/".join((self._make_url(), str(index), "resolve"))
+        else:
+            url = "/".join((self._make_url(), "resolve"))
+        return self._get(url=url, params=params)
+
+    def resolve_toolbox(
+        self,
+        index: Optional[int] = None,
+        tool_ids: Optional[List[str]] = None,
+        resolver_type: Optional[str] = None,

tpv is added, environments are removed, including local_env.

the mapping:
```
>       tools:
>         - class: local # these special tools that aren't parameterized for remote execution - expression tools, upload, etc
>           environment: local_env
```

needs to be removed at this point or data fetch jobs fail. Another mapping (testing tool to tpv_dispatcher) is added later in the tutorial.

Making this a draft PR as I'm not sure how to handle the diffs here and later on.  Can we leave the tools section empty like so?
```

I'm sure this has been noticed before, but I could not find an issue for it.

When singularity-enabled tools run for the first time they fail waiting for the image pull from biocontainers, with an obscure error:

```
FATAL:   Unable to handle docker://quay.io/biocontainers/gtdbtk:2.2.2--pyhdfd78af_0 uri: unable to create tmp file: open /mnt/singularity_data/tmp/sbuild-tmp-cache-171554111: no such file or directory
```

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: usegalaxy.org.au
Commit: 22.05

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Install a new tool to use the singularity runner (or delete the image for an existing one)
2. Try to run the tool for the first time 

**Expected behavior**
The tool should work first time following install or update. I couldn't locate the code that throws `unable to create tmp file` but at this point I would expect the code to wait until the image pull has completed.

Upon a fresh installation of the galaxy virtual environment, galaxy is broken due to this error:
`Exception: FilesSource plugin is missing required Python PyFilesystem2 plugin package [fs.dropboxfs]`

This isn't pinned and the latest version was installed (1.0.1) in which the DropboxFS is imported has changed. We fixed it by pip installing an older version, 0.2.2.post2.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version: release_22.05
Commit: (run `git rev-parse HEAD` if you run this Galaxy server)
